Overall:
Technologies that are at least partially effective for control of simazine include powdered activated carbon (PAC), granular activated carbon (GAC), ozonation, and membrane separation. GAC is the designated Best Available Technology for simazine control.  Chlorination, chlorine dioxide, conventional treatment and precipitative softening were not generally effective for removing simazine from drinking water. 
Control of deethylsimazine was observed in bench-scale studies with PAC.  Dethylsimazine was not contolled by ozonation, chlorination, conventional treatment or precipitative softening.  
Control of diaminochlorotriazine  was not observed in studies examining chlorination, conventional treatment or precipitative softening.
